commit.message:
fix: resolve stale closure chain in useMissions WebSocket message handling (#3424)

* Initial plan

* 🐛 fix: use refs to resolve stale closures in useMissions handleAgentMessage

- Add activeMissionIdRef and isSidebarOpenRef refs kept in sync with state
- Add handleAgentMessageRef to always hold the latest handler
- markMissionAsUnread reads from refs (stable [] deps) instead of state
- handleAgentMessage deps updated from [] to [markMissionAsUnread]
- ensureConnection onmessage calls handleAgentMessageRef.current to avoid stale closure

Fixes #134407106

Signed-off-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>

commit.message:
Simplify system configuration and service pricing

- Remove features field from tier_system_configurations table
  * Created migration script to drop features column
  * Updated TierSystemConfiguration TypeScript interface
  * Removed features UI from system config panel
  * Removed features from 'Apply to All' sync logic

- Simplify service pricing (HVAC Tune Up, etc.)
  * Services now show simple price + description interface
  * No tiers, warranty, or features complexity for services
  * Service data saved as single tier with description
  * Widget already handles single-price service display correctly

- Fix system config panel save button labels
  * Changed from 'Save Good/Better/Best' to just 'Save'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add no-console ESLint rule (#583)

Adds `no-console` rule to prevent accidental debugging statements in
production code.

## Changes

- Added `"no-console": "error"` to `.eslintrc.json` rules section

The rule enforces that console methods (log, warn, error, etc.) cannot
be used in linted source files. Build scripts and test configurations
remain unaffected as they fall outside the linting scope.

```json
"rules": {
  "react/react-in-jsx-scope": "off",
  "@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports": "error",
  "no-console": "error"
}
```

> [!WARNING]
>
> <details>
> <summary>Firewall rules blocked me from connecting to one or more
addresses (expand for details)</summary>
>
> #### I tried to connect to the following addresses, but was blocked by
firewall rules:
>
> - `download.cypress.io`
> - Triggering command: `/usr/local/bin/node node index.js --exec
install` (dns block)
>
> If you need me to access, download, or install something from one of
these locations, you can either:
>
> - Configure [Actions setup
steps](https://gh.io/copilot/actions-setup-steps) to set up my
environment, which run before the firewall is enabled
> - Add the appropriate URLs or hosts to the custom allowlist in this
repository's [Copilot coding agent
settings](https://github.com/mathiasbosman/mathiasbosman.me/settings/copilot/coding_agent)
(admins only)
>
> </details>

configuration in the `.eslintrc.json` file to prevent accidental

will enhance code quality by avoiding the unintentional use of
`console.log` in production.

commit.message:
Move UI concerns out of library

- Remove FORMAT_OPTIONS, memory helpers (getDefaultMemoryLimitMB,
  getDeviceMaxMemoryMB, MEMORY_STEP, MEMORY_MIN_MB) — these are
  consumer-side UI configuration
- Remove extractLabel and toGeoJSON from ExtentData — presentation
  logic belongs in the caller
- ExtentData.fetchExtents now returns raw nested data:
  rgExtents is { filename: { rg_N: bbox } } instead of flattened
- MetadataProvider.getParquetUrl is now identity (no .pmtiles/.mosaic.json
  assumption) — consumers subclass for custom URL schemes
- DefaultMetadataProvider.getMetaJsonUrl derives from getParquetUrl()
- Add WIP notice to README

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: geoparquet_extractor
repository.description: Library to help extract data from geoparquet in the browser

🐛 Fix multi-cluster: use deduplicatedClusters in MultiTenancy, i18n in ClusterSelect (#3356)

* Initial plan

* 🐛 Fix multi-cluster: use deduplicatedClusters in MultiTenancy and i18n in ClusterSelect

- MultiTenancy.tsx: Use deduplicatedClusters instead of raw clusters to
  avoid double-counting when multiple kubeconfig contexts point to the
  same server (consistent with AIML.tsx pattern)
- ClusterSelect.tsx: Replace hardcoded English state labels with t()
  i18n calls (consistent with ClusterFilterDropdown.tsx)

commit.message:
🐛 Fix Button ARIA accessibility: aria-busy and aria-hidden for loading state (#3375)

* Initial plan

* 🐛 Fix Button ARIA accessibility: add aria-busy and aria-hidden for loading state

- Add aria-busy={loading ? true : undefined} to button element so screen
  readers announce the button is processing (WCAG SC 4.1.3)
- Add aria-hidden="true" to loading spinner span to exclude it from the
  accessibility tree (button aria-busy already communicates loading state)
- Add tests covering aria-busy, aria-hidden on spinner, and aria-label
  derivation rules

Fixes #(issue)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

Harden atomic temp file handling

Support Python 3.14 in dependency lockfile

commit.message:
[Tooltips] Fix positioning to never overflow viewport (#13230)

### This PR improves tooltip behavior: better positioning, smarter
viewport handling, and some minor cleanup.

**Viewport-aware positioning**
> Added a `clampToViewport` function that keeps tooltips from spilling
outside the screen. It flips the tooltip to the opposite side if needed,
and nudges it back within the viewport margins before applying the
position.

**Removal and event handling**
> `removeTooltips` now has a guard clause so it won't throw if the
tooltip is already gone. The `mouseenter` handler is simplified, and
tooltips are properly cleaned up on navigation and frame loads.

**Cleanup**
> Removed some stale comments and tightened up the class sync logic when
showing tooltips.

---------

Co-authored-by: Manu Gurudath <manu@Manus-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  PlanningFollowup, Refresh, Starvation, Throughput): cover redirect
  behavior when cookies missing and full rendering when cookies present

Result: 94% line coverage, 83% branch coverage (threshold: 80%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Address PR #1856 review feedback: fix fail-open, tree-ish parsing, pathspec-from-file

Fix three issues identified in code review:

1. HIGH: AddStagedFilesToModifiedPaths now returns failure when
   git diff --cached exits non-zero, instead of silently succeeding
   with addedCount=0 (which re-opened the corruption this PR fixes).

2. MEDIUM: GetRestorePathspec now strips the tree-ish argument for
   checkout commands (e.g. HEAD in 'checkout HEAD -- file'), and
   skips --source/-s values for restore commands, preventing
   accidental pathspec scope widening.

3. MEDIUM: --pathspec-from-file and --pathspec-file-nul are now
   forwarded through the IPC protocol to the mount process, which
   passes them directly to git diff --cached. The hook never reads
   the file itself -- git handles all pathspec parsing on both sides.
   Stdin (-) is unsupported in hook context and fails closed.

Refactors parsing logic into UnstageCommandParser (public static class
in a separate file) so it can be linked into GVFS.UnitTests without
pulling in the full GVFS.Hooks Exe assembly.

DiffCachedNameStatus now accepts optional pathspecFromFile and
pathspecFileNul parameters.

IPC message body format extended:
  null/empty        -> all staged files
  path1\0path2      -> inline pathspecs (unchanged)
  \nF\n<filepath>   -> --pathspec-from-file
  \nFZ\n<filepath>  -> --pathspec-from-file + --pathspec-file-nul

Adds 28 unit tests covering IsUnstageOperation and GetRestorePathspec.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🐛 Fix error severity: use console.error instead of console.warn for fetch failures in workload hooks (#3338)

* Initial plan

* 🐛 Fix swallowed errors: use console.error instead of console.warn for fetch failures

Change console.warn to console.error in catch blocks for actual fetch
failures in useDeployments, useHPAs, useReplicaSets, useStatefulSets,
useDaemonSets, and useCronJobs hooks. These represent genuine error
conditions and should be logged at error severity for proper visibility
in monitoring tools and browser devtools.

Signed-off-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>

Co-authored-by: clubanderson <407614+clubanderson@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/kubestellar/console/sessions/f4c3f32b-d3da-4023-a17a-378a352f9045

* 🐛 Include original err object in console.error calls for full stack traces

Pass the original err object as the third argument to all six
console.error calls in workload hook catch blocks, ensuring stack
traces and structured error details are preserved in devtools/monitoring.

Signed-off-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>

repository.name: tiny-link
repository.description: TinyLink is a lightweight, template-based serial protocol for reliable UART communication between microcontrollers. It features a robust state machine, checksum validation, and a hardware-agnostic design ideal for linking resource-constrained devices like the MH-Tiny88 and ESP-M3. Supports custom structs, error tracking, and non-blocking parsing.

repository.name: pkgskills
repository.description: AI Skills for R Package Development

commit.message:
test(game): add fuzz tests with randomized configs

50 random full-game simulations with varying player
count (2-6), map shape, grid type, fog of war,
power-ups, alliances, and personality mixes including
custom configs. Plus 7 edge case tests. Seeds printed
on failure for reproducibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ai): add custom AI personality builder

Add ability to create custom AI personalities with adjustable parameters:
- Min Advantage slider (-2 to +4)
- Max Attacks slider (1 to 10, or unlimited)
- Connectivity Bonus slider (0 to 5)

Includes named preset save/load/delete via localStorage, popup editor
in SetupScene, and full integration with GameScene player creation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(game): change Charge power-up from flat +3 bonus to +2 extra dice

Charge now adds 2 extra dice to the attacker's roll instead of a flat +3
to the total. This lets territories with 8 dice roll 10 dice on attack,
making Charge meaningfully powerful at high dice counts.

- Rename POWER_UP_BONUS to CHARGE_EXTRA_DICE (2) and SHIELD_DEFENSE_BONUS (3)
- Roll extra dice before summing instead of adding to total after
- Update AI advantage calculation to use +2 for charge
- Shield remains as flat +3 defender bonus (passive defense)
- Add new tests for extra dice rolling and power-up consumption
- Update GAME_LOGIC.md documentation
- Replace simulation seed 123 (stalemates under new balance)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: mark phase-11 defect ticket as done

All 8 bugs fixed: seed degeneration, self-alliance,
duplicate elimination, dead alliance cleanup,
graph perf, surrender awareness, underdog logic,
undo alliance state. Plus undo config feature.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(game): make undo configurable in game setup

Gate undo functionality on undoEnabled config:
- Skip snapshot creation when undo is disabled
- Hide undo button and ignore Z key when disabled
- Remove undo shortcut from help overlay when disabled
- Add tests for undoEnabled persistence and validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(game): AI surrender considers power-ups and alliances

hasPositiveAdvantageAttack() now accounts for charge power-up (+2
effective attack dice) and filters out allied targets that the AI
personality would not attack. This prevents premature surrender when
the AI has meaningful tactical options via power-ups or when alliance
restrictions falsely suggest no valid targets exist.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(game): prevent duplicate elimination events on human attacks

Capture alive players before executeAttack() and only fire elimination
events for players who were alive before but are now dead. This matches
the pattern already used in AI turn processing and prevents corrupted
achievement tracking (triple_kill, first_blood).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
